# Docker non-sudo execution (2026-01-15 17:13Z)

_**User (2026-01-15 05:41Z)**_

command to make docker run without sudo

---

_**Agent (model composer-1, mode Agent)**_



---


To run Docker without `sudo` on Linux, add your user to the `docker` group:

```bash
sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
```

Then log out and back in (or restart your terminal session) for the change to take effect.

Verify it works:
```bash
docker run hello-world
```

**Note:** On macOS (Docker Desktop), `sudo` usually isn't needed. If you're seeing this on macOS, ensure Docker Desktop is running and your user has the necessary permissions.

**Security Note:** Adding a user to the `docker` group grants privileges equivalent to root, since Docker can access the host system. Only add trusted users to this group.

If you're on macOS and still need `sudo`, share the exact error message and we can troubleshoot.

_**User (2026-01-15 05:41Z)**_

ngrok installation ubuntu command

---

_**Agent (model composer-1, mode Agent)**_



---


Install ngrok on Ubuntu:

**Method 1: Using snap (recommended)**
```bash
sudo snap install ngrok
```

**Method 2: Download binary directly**
```bash
# Download ngrok
curl -s https://ngrok-agent.s3.amazonaws.com/ngrok.asc | sudo tee /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/ngrok.asc >/dev/null && echo "deb https://ngrok-agent.s3.amazonaws.com buster main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ngrok.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install ngrok
```

**Method 3: Manual download**
```bash
# Download the latest Linux binary
wget https://bin.equinox.io/c/bNyj1mQVY4c/ngrok-v3-stable-linux-amd64.tgz

# Extract
tar -xzf ngrok-v3-stable-linux-amd64.tgz

# Move to a directory in your PATH (optional)
sudo mv ngrok /usr/local/bin/
```

After installation, authenticate with your ngrok authtoken:
```bash
ngrok config add-authtoken YOUR_AUTHTOKEN
```

Get your authtoken from: https://dashboard.ngrok.com/get-started/your-authtoken

**Quick test:**
```bash
ngrok http 3000
```

Method 1 (snap) is usually the simplest.
